name: SEO Keywords
description: build a keyword set with intent and difficulty for a target page or topic
when_to_use: when the user wants keywords to target, an seo plan, or to know what to rank for
body:
1. clarify the page, product, and who's searching for it.
2. seed 5-8 head terms, then expand into long-tail + question variants.
3. tag each by intent (informational/commercial/transactional) and rough difficulty.
4. group into clusters, pick one primary per page + 3-5 secondaries.
5. output a table: keyword, intent, difficulty, cluster, target url.

name: Social Post
description: write a platform-native short post with hook, body, and cta
when_to_use: when the user wants a tweet, linkedin, instagram, or other social post
body:
1. nail the platform + goal (reach, clicks, replies) before writing.
2. open with a scroll-stopping first line, no throat-clearing.
3. keep body to one idea, match the platform's voice and length.
4. end with a single clear cta or question.
5. add 2-4 relevant hashtags only if the platform rewards them.

name: Landing Copy
description: write conversion-focused landing page copy section by section
when_to_use: when the user needs copy for a landing or sales page
body:
1. pin the one audience, one offer, one desired action.
2. write a benefit-led headline + subhead that names the outcome.
3. draft sections: problem, solution, how-it-works, proof, faq, cta.
4. lead with benefits, back each with a concrete feature or number.
5. repeat the cta after value is built; keep it the same verb throughout.

name: Newsletter
description: draft an email newsletter issue with subject, hook, and sections
when_to_use: when the user wants to write or plan an email newsletter issue
body:
1. decide the one takeaway the reader should leave with.
2. write 2-3 subject lines + a preview line, optimize for opens not clicks.
3. open personal and direct, get to value in the first two lines.
4. structure as scannable sections with bold mini-headers.
5. close with one cta and a p.s. that restates the hook.

name: Ad Variations
description: generate multiple ad copy variants for testing across angles
when_to_use: when the user needs ad copy, ppc variants, or creatives to a/b test
body:
1. confirm platform, character limits, and the single conversion goal.
2. list 3-4 distinct angles (pain, gain, social proof, curiosity).
3. write 2 variants per angle: each with headline, primary text, cta.
4. keep one variable changing at a time so tests stay readable.
5. label every variant by angle so results map back cleanly.

name: Content Calendar
description: plan a dated content schedule across channels and themes
when_to_use: when the user wants a posting schedule, editorial calendar, or content plan
body:
1. set the timeframe, channels, and cadence per channel.
2. define 3-5 recurring themes or pillars to rotate through.
3. map each slot to date, channel, theme, format, and working title.
4. balance the mix (educate/entertain/promote) so it's not all selling.
5. output as a table sorted by date, leave a notes column for status.

name: Value Prop
description: distill a sharp value proposition and one-line positioning
when_to_use: when the user needs a value proposition, positioning, or one-liner for a product
body:
1. gather target customer, their problem, and the alternatives.
2. fill 'for [who] who [need], [product] is a [category] that [benefit].'
3. name the one differentiator a competitor can't easily copy.
4. compress to a single line under 12 words, no jargon.
5. give 2-3 backup variants at different specificity levels.

name: Blog Outline
description: structure an seo-aware blog post outline before drafting
when_to_use: when the user wants to outline an article or blog post
body:
1. lock the primary keyword and the searcher's actual question.
2. write a working title + angle that beats what already ranks.
3. lay out h2/h3 sections answering the question top to bottom.
4. note key points, data, or examples to drop under each section.
5. plan intro hook and a cta, flag any internal links to add.

name: Brand Voice
description: define a reusable brand voice guide with do/don't examples
when_to_use: when the user wants to define, document, or audit their brand voice or tone
body:
1. collect existing copy + a few brands they admire or hate.
2. pick 3-4 voice traits, each as an adjective plus a 'but not' guardrail.
3. set rules for person, tense, formality, humor, and forbidden words.
4. show one sample sentence rewritten on-voice vs off-voice.
5. condense to a one-page cheat sheet anyone can apply.

name: Product Description
description: write ecommerce product copy that sells on benefits and specs
when_to_use: when the user needs product page or catalog descriptions
body:
1. list the buyer, their objection, and the standout feature.
2. open with a benefit-led line that paints the outcome.
3. translate each spec into what it does for the buyer.
4. add scannable bullets for specs, sizing, materials, what's in box.
5. close with a reassurance line (returns, guarantee) and a cta.

name: Email Sequence
description: map a multi-email nurture or onboarding drip with goals per send
when_to_use: when the user wants a drip campaign, welcome series, or nurture sequence
body:
1. define the entry trigger, the end goal, and the time window.
2. plan 4-6 emails, one job each (welcome, value, proof, offer, nudge).
3. set send timing/delay between each and the exit condition.
4. for every email: subject, one-line goal, core message, cta.
5. add a fallback for non-openers and note where to segment.

name: Repurpose Content
description: turn one source asset into multiple channel-specific pieces
when_to_use: when the user wants to repurpose, atomize, or reuse existing content across channels
body:
1. read the source asset and pull its 3-5 strongest standalone points.
2. pick target channels and the format each one wants.
3. reshape each point into a native piece (thread, clip script, carousel, snippet).
4. rewrite per channel voice and length, don't just copy-paste.
5. list each output with channel, format, and a hook line.

name: Headline Test
description: generate and rank multiple headline options against a goal
when_to_use: when the user wants headline, title, or subject line options to choose between
body:
1. confirm the asset, audience, and whether the goal is opens or clicks.
2. write 8-10 headlines spanning angles (benefit, curiosity, number, how-to, fear).
3. keep each tight, front-load the strongest word, kill filler.
4. score each on clarity, specificity, and emotional pull.
5. recommend the top 2-3 and say why they win.

name: Competitor Teardown
description: analyze a competitor's messaging and surface positioning gaps
when_to_use: when the user wants to analyze a competitor's marketing, copy, or positioning
body:
1. pull their homepage hero, value prop, pricing framing, and main cta.
2. note their target audience, tone, and the proof they lean on.
3. list what they do well and where the messaging is weak or vague.
4. find the gap or angle they're not claiming.
5. recommend 3 concrete moves to differentiate against them.

name: Press Release
description: draft a newsworthy press release with a tight inverted-pyramid structure
when_to_use: when the user needs to announce a launch, milestone, or news item to media or the public
body:
1. confirm the news angle, embargo date, and the one quote to anchor it.
2. write a headline that works as a standalone news sentence.
3. lead paragraph: who, what, when, where, why in under 40 words.
4. body: add context, data, and a spokesperson quote in inverted-pyramid order.
5. close with a boilerplate about the company and media contact details.

name: Case Study
description: write a before-after customer story that sells through proof
when_to_use: when the user wants to turn a customer win into a marketing case study or success story
body:
1. gather customer name, vertical, problem, solution used, and measurable results.
2. structure as: situation, complication, action, result (scar).
3. open with the result so the reader knows payoff is coming.
4. back every claim with a specific number or quote.
5. close with a pull quote and a one-line cta to try the product.

name: Tagline Options
description: generate punchy tagline candidates with rationale
when_to_use: when the user needs a slogan, catchphrase, or tagline for a brand, product, or campaign
body:
1. lock the audience, core feeling, and the one thing the brand must own.
2. draft 10 taglines spanning styles (action, outcome, personality, provocation).
3. keep each under 7 words, no jargon, no clichés.
4. cut to the top 5, add a one-line rationale for each.
5. flag which works best for ads vs brand-building vs spoken pitch.

name: Campaign Brief
description: write a one-page creative brief that aligns a campaign before production starts
when_to_use: when the user is kicking off a campaign and needs to align on goals, audience, and message before producing assets
body:
1. fill the 5 slots: business goal, audience, single-minded message, tone, and success metric.
2. write a one-line campaign idea sentence a creative team can react to.
3. list deliverables, channels, and timeline at a glance.
4. name what's in-scope and explicitly what's out-of-scope.
5. flag open decisions and who owns each one.

name: Video Script
description: write a short-form or explainer video script with scene and voiceover
when_to_use: when the user needs a script for a product video, explainer, reel, or youtube ad
body:
1. set format: length in seconds, style (talking-head/animation/screen-record), goal.
2. write a hook in the first 3 seconds that earns the next 30.
3. structure as problem → demo/insight → payoff, one idea per scene.
4. write visual directions in [brackets], keep vo tight-1 word/second target.
5. close with a spoken cta and hold a visual slate with link or offer.

name: Testimonial Polish
description: edit raw customer quotes into punchy, credible testimonials without fabricating
when_to_use: when the user has raw customer feedback and wants to turn it into usable testimonials
body:
1. read the raw quote without editing intent-note the strongest authentic claim.
2. cut filler words and rambles, keep the customer's own vocabulary.
3. tighten to 1-3 sentences max, lead with the specific result.
4. if a number is vague, flag it for the customer to confirm, don't invent one.
5. add a display name + role + company line beneath each polished quote.

name: Referral Program
description: design a referral program structure with incentives and messaging
when_to_use: when the user wants to build or improve a referral, word-of-mouth, or affiliate program
body:
1. decide the reward structure: one-sided vs two-sided, and what the reward is.
2. set the sharing moment-when in the user journey to surface it.
3. draft the invite message the referrer sends (short, personal, benefit-first).
4. write the landing copy the referred friend sees after clicking.
5. define tracking, payout trigger, and fraud limit before launch.

name: Podcast Pitch
description: write a personalised podcast guest pitch that gets a reply
when_to_use: when the user wants to pitch themselves or a client as a podcast guest
body:
1. listen to or read notes on a recent episode to nail the show's angle.
2. open by referencing something specific from that episode-not generic praise.
3. introduce the guest in one line: who they are and what they've done.
4. pitch 2-3 episode angle ideas with a concrete talking-point for each.
5. keep the email under 150 words and end with a single soft ask.

name: Objection Responses
description: write sales copy or scripts that preempt and handle common buyer objections
when_to_use: when the user wants to address customer objections in copy, a sales deck, or a faq section
body:
1. list the top 5-7 objections buyers raise (price, trust, timing, alternatives, complexity).
2. for each, acknowledge the concern before countering it.
3. reframe price objections with cost-of-inaction or roi math.
4. handle trust with specific proof: stats, guarantees, logos, named customers.
5. output each as a question-answer pair usable in faq, deck, or call script.

name: Launch Checklist
description: build a pre-launch marketing checklist covering every channel and asset
when_to_use: when the user is planning a product launch, campaign go-live, or content drop and needs to make sure nothing is missed
body:
1. set the launch date and work backwards to identify deadline dependencies.
2. list asset categories: copy, visuals, email, social, paid, pr, site.
3. under each, itemize every piece needed with owner and done-by date.
4. add a 48-hour pre-launch sanity check row: links, tracking pixels, legal sign-off.
5. include a day-of monitor list and a rollback plan if something breaks.
